title: Sleep Comes When I Stop Chasing It
subtitle: An affirmation for releasing the effort that blocks sleep
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 22 of a 30-day sleep program. The statement names the central paradox of effortful sleep: the body already knows what to do, and stopping the chase is what allows it.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 22 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short affirmation for placing sleep back in the body rather than in the effort. The statement names what is already true: the body knows how to sleep. The practice is the release of trying.
My body already knows how to sleep. Sleep is natural, not complicated. Sleep comes when I stop chasing it.
Say it three times, slow. Let the third repetition land without adding anything after it. If the mind wants to question it, that is the habit the affirmation is working against, keep going.
Adapted from Day 22 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
